pattern: Dynamic programming tracks both the longest increasing subsequence length ending at each index and how many such subsequences exist, combining counts when equal lengths extend.
hints: For each index keep the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence that ends there.
Alongside the length, keep a count of how many distinct subsequences achieve that length ending at the index.

When extending j after a smaller earlier i: if it gives a strictly longer length adopt that length and copy the count, but if it ties the current best length add the earlier count; finally sum counts over all indices at the global maximum length.

edge_inputs.description: all equal so longest length is one with count equal to size
strictly increasing has one longest subsequence
negatives and a tie that must merge counts
duplicates interleaved so equal values do not extend

stress.description: random array over a narrow range so many equal-length subsequences appear
stress.note: narrow range maximises ties to stress count merging
